summary: Tag each Agricola card with the mechanics/features it uses

cards.note: On play, optionally build 1 free stable (granted sub-action) restricted to a 1-cell pasture (a per-pasture size restriction, not adjacency geometry); passing minor.
On play, random draw among 3 chosen hand occupations (L-RANDOM), then play it free (granted play-a-card sub-action); if its extra cost is unpayable it is removed from game; passing minor.
Play cost is a choice (2 Food OR 1 Grain -> E-ALTCOST; food option -> E-FOODCOST); on play gains wood from a plain state read (rooms, rooms>people); passing minor.
On play, immediate goods conditional on a plain state read (which majors you own, max 1 each); passing minor.
On play, immediate food/grain from a plain read of field contents (no crop manipulation); passing minor.
Prereq is a plain HAVE-check (no tag); on play +1 grain +1 veg; passing minor.

Playable ONLY via a Major Improvement action (extends that action's eligibility to this minor; not via House Redevelopment); provides a room-for-one-person (new person holder, E-CAPNEW); permanent passive rule change: renovation banned thereafter.
On play +1 boar (must be accommodated); ongoing passive rule creating new animal slots: 1 boar per unplanted field tile.
On play +3 clay, then an optional (declinable, per clarification) granted renovate sub-action with zero resource cost; the free cost is part of the grant, not a persistent cost modifier.
Always-on cost modifier: whenever building >=2 rooms at once, discount of 2 reed + 2 wood/3 clay/4 stone by house material.
Fires after using any wood accumulation space, condition >=7 wood in supply (plain state read); optional granted build-1-stable at the grant's own 1-wood cost.

One-shot ('the next time', once ever) after an animal-accumulation-space take where ALL taken animals are accommodated — needs the take/accommodation payload (ST-PROV); non-qualifying takes neither trigger nor void; optional granted plow.
Once per game, 'when you use' Farmland/Cultivation (default T-BEFORE) with the round's first placed person (computable from board state); optional +2 granted plows.

cards.new_category: COST-BY-ROUND: the card's printed play cost varies deterministically with the round number it is played in (1 grain, +1 food if round 4-5), not a player choice
cards.note: On play, optionally plow up to 2 fields (granted sub-action); round-limited play window is a prerequisite; the round-4/5 surcharge is food (E-FOODCOST) and round-dependent rather than a chosen alt-cost.

Fires on your Build Rooms sub-action (default T-BEFORE per 'each time' ruling; condition 'more rooms than people already' is a pre-check) and grants an optional Family Growth (exactly 1 per clarification) plus 1 food; normal growth, no 'without room' wording.

cards.new_category: SCHED-EFFECT: schedule a non-goods ability on a specific future round space (mark the round; the ability activates only in that round)
cards.note: 2-food play cost; marks round = current + fences-in-supply (ST-STORE) and in that round only lets you place a temporary person from your supply (returned in returning-home) — an extra temporary worker; E-SCHED covers only goods, so the marked-round ability is a new category.

'Immediately after' using Quarry -> T-AFTER; grants a full Major-or-Minor Improvement action constrained to spend >=1 stone; tagged F-TRIG per granted-actions-optional default, though the clarification limits declining it (not declinable to enable Field Merchant).

Once per work phase you may place an extra person on the (occupied-by-1) first non-accumulating space used by any player — needs a log of placement order to identify that specific space (marked per clarification).
On play: +2 wood plus an optional immediate build of Clay/Stone Oven major at a replacement cost of 1 clay + 1 stone.
Two always-on rules: treat Lessons spaces as unoccupied, and substitute wood for each food in occupation play-costs (payment-currency substitution = E-COSTMOD).
Each returning-home phase you may pay exactly 1 grain for 1 bonus point (action-earned E-SCORE, running counter); doing so gives each other player 1 food (gift transfer).
On each of your Bake Bread sub-actions (must bake normally; 'each time' default T-BEFORE) you may exchange exactly 1 grain for 2 food + 1 bonus point.
2-food play cost; pure end-game scoring rule (1 pt per major improvement capped by unused farmyard spaces) — plain count/min, computed at scoring, no hook, not E-SCOREOPT.
Fires on your build-fences sub-action ('each time' default T-BEFORE); optional exchange of 1 wood + 1 fence piece from supply (piece cost) for 2 food + 1 bonus point.
Automatic in each returning-home phase: 2 bonus pts per newborn returned home, conditional on a person returning from Fishing — needs which-worker-on-which-space plus newborn classification.
One-time on play: exchange any number of food (food-paid effect) for 1 bonus point each, capped by completed harvests (plain state read); bonus points stored on the card.
On each of your renovate sub-actions ('each time' default T-BEFORE) you may pay 1 wood for 1 bonus point + 1 grain.
The card is a shared action space; any user automatically gets 3 bonus points, and another player using it must FIRST (T-BEFORE) pay the owner 1 grain (opponent-to-owner transfer).

cards.new_category: FARM-GOODS: goods stored on individual farmyard cells, released when that cell changes state (becomes used)
cards.note: On play seeds clay onto each unused farmyard cell; any sub-action that converts a cell to used (plow/room/stable/fence) auto-releases the clay, with an optional immediate 1-clay->2-food exchange (that part F-TRIG-like). Needs per-cell goods storage, not covered by ST-STACK (on-card).

Fires on the specific event 'upgrade Fireplace -> Cooking Hearth' (a return-route improvement build, reachable via Major/Minor Improvement and other improvement-build entry points); needs provenance of which improvement was built and that a Fireplace was returned. Auto-gain 2 wood + 1 veg.
'Each time you use' Fishing/Forest -> before-space hook, auto extra 1 wood/food matched to the space used.

Build-stable sub-action hook, but fires at most once per turn regardless of how many stables built (needs a fired-this-turn flag); clarification restricts to on-turn builds. Schedules 1 food on next 3 round spaces.
One-shot latch on the standing condition 'no longer in a wooden house' (any renovation away from wood); schedules 1 food on the next 6 round spaces.
'Each time you use' Sheep Market -> before-space hook; schedules 1 food on each of the next 2 round spaces.
One-time on-play scheduling of food onto future round spaces, count = fences built (plain state read).

Fires after obtaining wood; conversion 1 wood -> 3 food is optional at 5-6 wood (F-TRIG) but mandatory at 7+ (F-AUTO). Clarification allows multiple firings per simultaneous-gain batch only if interleaved with another effect.

Triggered by the preparation-phase board refill (a nature event, not a player action) — fires when reed is added to an already-non-empty Reed Bank; auto 1 food. A-OWN tagged per default since no opponent action is involved.
'Each time you use' Fishing -> before-space hook, auto +2 food.
Before-hook on any wood accumulation space, conditioned on a plain board-state read (>=1 boar sitting on Pig Market); auto +2 food.

+5 food on play; then a mandatory end-of-round hook (skipping harvest rounds): pay 1 food OR take a begging marker — mandatory with a choice, and the food payment is E-FOODCOST. Negative begging-marker outcome noted.
One-shot on-play food gain scaled by plain animal-count reads (sheep and cattle tiers); trivially implementable.

Optional returning-home hook in fixed rounds 8/10/12 (fixed round numbers, not geometry): remove 1 veg from a field (crop manipulation), optionally converting it to 3 food + 1 bonus point (action-earned bonus points need a running counter).

Fires after harvesting 1+ veg (field phase or card-effect harvest per clarification); optional at 3+ veg (F-TRIG), mandatory at 4+ (F-AUTO) — firing kind is threshold-dependent. Converts exactly 1 veg into 6 food.

cards.new_category: DUALTYPE: the card counts as either 1 minor or 1 major improvement, whichever is most convenient for another effect (never both at once)
cards.note: At-any-time cooking conversions (veg/sheep/cattle) + a bake-bread rate (grain→2 food = E-BAKESPEC). Play cost is returning a Fireplace/Cooking Hearth (E-RETURNCOMP), and it can itself be returned to pay for a Cooking Hearth (L-EXT: a new legal return target).

After each harvest's field phase, optionally convert exactly 1 grain to food at an owned baking improvement's rate; explicitly NOT a Bake Bread action, so it's a hook-granted conversion, not E-GRANTSUB.

During each feeding phase, optionally exchange 1/2/3 grain for 0/1/2 bonus points + exactly 3 food — bonus points earned through an action legitimately combine HOOK + E-SCORE. Timed within feeding so the food can pay feeding (T-BEFORE the feed resolution).
Automatic in each harvest field phase: +1 food per grain field harvested (a per-field count read at field-phase resolution).
Before each unconditional Sow sub-action, automatically gain 1 grain (so it can be sown); 'unconditional' restricts which Sow grants qualify.
'Each time you use [an animal accumulation space]' → T-BEFORE; condition = already own an animal of that type (clarification: Animal Dealer may resolve first). Gain 1 grain, automatic.
After building fences, if the number built in that action ≥ current round number, immediately gain 1 vegetable; the round comparison is a plain state read.
At the end of each non-harvest round, optionally move 1 vegetable from a field to supply — crop manipulation on fields, explicitly not a field phase.
'Each time you use a Quarry accumulation space' → T-BEFORE; automatically adds 1 matching crop onto each single-crop planted field (manipulating field contents, not loose goods).

After any action that converts 2+ unused farmyard spaces to used (plow/rooms/stables/fences), grants an optional Sow sub-action; needs a per-action count of spaces converted (ST-COUNTER). Trigger spans multiple sub-action types.
After building 1+ stables ON YOUR TURN (clarification: off-turn builds via other cards don't trigger), schedule 1 wood on each of the next 3 round spaces.
At the start of each work phase (≈ start of round, S-SOR), if holding 1+ clay, optionally exchange exactly 1 grain for 2 clay + 1 food.
On play +1 clay; then 'each time ANY player uses Pig Market' (A-OWN + A-OPP) automatically gain 2 clay — space-use hook, T-BEFORE per the each-time-you-use default.
Fires on own sow sub-action, conditional on the sow's content (>=1 veg planted, and the sow being 'unconditional'), so it needs the sow payload and resolves after; mandatory goods gain.

Two hooks: on using a clay/reed/stone accumulation space, mandatorily place 1 wood/clay/reed (a choice) on the card (goods pile on card = ST-STACK); at start of rounds 7/11/14 the pile auto-moves to supply.
After each own action-space use, may take 1 building resource from any accumulation space holding >=4 (removes it from the board space); clarification: can trigger immediately on play.
Optional breed outside harvest in each non-harvest returning-home phase; grain cost may be paid from a planted field (crop removal), yields one newborn of a chosen type.
On play grants a plow sub-action (optional per granted-sub-action rule), then passes to the other player.
Immediate clay gain from a plain state read (floor(clay/2)), then passes; trivially implementable apart from passing.
Immediate 1 cattle (must be accommodated); sheep play-cost is an ordinary animal cost; then passes.
Always-on +2 capacity on every existing pasture.
On play +1 clay; ongoing passive substitution of clay for wood when paying for fences (mixable within one Build Fences action).
On play schedules a FIELD TILE (a piece, not goods — nearest code E-SCHED) onto round space current+5; at that round's start an optional plow of that field fires.
'Each time you use' Farmland/Cultivation (default T-BEFORE) grants an optional additional Bake Bread sub-action.
Always-on: Wish for Children spaces usable when occupied by exactly one other person (not 2+).
Plain end-game threshold/coverage bonus (pasture coverage 6/7/8/10 -> 1/2/3/4 pts); no optimization, no hook.

cards.new_category: IMMEDIATE-VP: bonus victory points awarded during play at the moment the card resolves (a running bonus-point tally), not a scoring-time rule
cards.note: On play, per complete round remaining: 2 food (E-GOODS) plus 1 bonus point awarded immediately — E-SCORE is defined as an end-game rule, so the immediate VP gain needs a new category; prereq is just a play condition.
End-game bonus rule keyed on house material at scoring; plain state read, no hook.
On play, schedules 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces.
Fires on ANY player's use of Cattle Market (both actors); mandatory food to owner AND 1 food to each other player (goods granted to opponents).
Returning-home check needs a running count of building resources gained during the work phase (clarification: gross gains, excluding start-of-round gains).
Fires after building ANY improvement (major build or minor card play, any entry point), including itself.

cards.new_category: SPACE-DEPOSIT: place goods from your supply back onto an action space's accumulation pile
cards.note: Optional 2 wood -> 3 food immediately after any wood accumulation space; the paid wood is deposited back onto that space, not the supply.
Bonus food on Bake Bread sub-actions; the 'round following a harvest' condition is fixed rounds (5/8/10/12/14), computable, no geometry.
+1 grain each use of Grain Seeds.
Schedules 1 vegetable on round spaces current+4/+7/+9.
'At any time' move 1 crop between fields — deferrable crop-on-field manipulation.
Always-on: every improvement costs 1 wood less; occupation-count prereq is just a play condition.
+1 food +1 reed each use of Fishing.
+1 stone each use of any stone accumulation space (class of spaces, not one named space).

Fires on the fence sub-action creating a new undivided pasture of >=4 cells; needs the just-built pasture's identity/size (provenance) and places 2 sheep on it (animals must be accommodated). Size is a count, not adjacency geometry.
On play, a mandatory restricted build-fences sub-action (one new enclosure, wood-free); adjacency-to-existing-pastures mirrors normal fencing connectivity, not new geometry; passes after resolving.
Immediate 1 vegetable for a 1-grain play cost; traveling card passed on after play.
The card itself is a new holder providing room for 1 person (clarified: not a room), raising person capacity continuously.
Always-on replacement cost formula for wooden rooms (2 wood + 2 reed each).
On play +1 food; each renovation (sub-action) optionally grants ('you can also', after the renovation resolves) a wood-free build-stable sub-action.

On play stocks 2 field tiles on the card (a consumable pile); each use of Farmland ('each time you use' = before) optionally grants an extra plow from the card, limited to twice per game (the 2 tiles).

Optional back-to-back second placement tied to using an animal-market space; timing is a turn-structure tweak (either placement may be the market one), tagged as an optional own-action space hook granting the extra placement.
On play +1 food; after each occupation you play, grants an additional Bake Bread sub-action (granted sub-actions are optional).

cards.new_category: L-RESTRICT: permanently removes/forbids a normally-legal action (here: renovation) for the owner
cards.note: Bonus points fixed at play time (1 per complete round left — must be stored), plus a permanent negative rule change banning renovation, which no legality code captures.
No effect beyond its play cost; the cost is state-computed (1 clay + 1 food per person, newborns included) and includes food, which may need conversion to pay.
Automatic food each harvest field phase on sheep thresholds (1/4/7 -> 1/2/3), plus a passive end-game bonus rule (1 pt per 3 sheep) — scoring is computed, not hooked.
On play, schedules 1 food onto each of the next 3 round spaces.
'Each time you use Fishing' fires before the space's action (default ruling), mandatorily scheduling 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces.
Automatic food in each harvest field phase based on current sheep (per 3) and cattle (per 2) counts.

"Each time you use" space hook (before, auto, own) granting food; 'the four spaces above Fishing' depends on board arrangement (L-GEOMBOARD); play-cost requires knowing one of YOUR people sits on Fishing (ST-PLACELOG).
Start-of-round auto hook, conditional on a plain state read (wooden house), gains 1 food.
Fires at the start of each harvest's field phase (before it resolves); condition is a plain read of field contents; gains 3 food.
"Each time you use Grain Seeds" = before-space auto hook; reading a NAMED space's occupancy (Farmland) is a plain state read, not board geometry.
On play, schedules 1 grain onto fixed future round spaces (5/8/11/14); fixed round numbers are not board geometry.
The card is itself a field (vegetables only) — fully captured by L-CARDFIELD as a continuous rule; no EFFECT code applies (it neither gains goods nor grants an action itself).
Schedules 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space (fixed rounds); the '5 Clay in Your Supply' line is a have-check prerequisite-style condition, no mechanic.
"Each time you use Day Laborer" = before-space auto hook, +3 clay; the Cottager clarification confirms the clay is available before/during the action (T-BEFORE).
"At any time" deferrable exchange of clay for stone at a stepped rate.
Schedules wild boar onto 2 round spaces; the boar arrive later and must be accommodated (E-ANIMALS).
On play: 5 clay immediately plus an optional granted Renovation sub-action at normal renovation cost; passing minor.

cards.new_category: HANDCARD-TRANSFER: give a card from your hand to another player (the randomly drawn occupation goes to the left neighbor if not played)
cards.note: Random draw from own hand (L-RANDOM); mandatory choice: play it as an occupation for a 2-food cost (E-FOODCOST) or transfer the hand card left — the card-to-opponent transfer isn't goods, hence the new category; itself a passing minor.
Immediate wood equal to your people on accumulation spaces — needs to know which spaces your workers occupy (ST-PLACELOG); passing minor.
Immediate single good chosen by a plain read of occupations left in hand; trivially implementable apart from being a passing minor.
One-shot stone gain scaled by person count (plain state read; newborns count); 2-food play cost may need conversion; passing minor.
Immediate food scaled by owned bottom-row majors (plain state read); passing minor.
On play, optional choice: gain 1 reed OR exchange 1 reed for 1 cattle (a conversion; cattle must be accommodated); passing minor.
Card is a new animal holder with dynamic capacity (1 per pasture, mixed types); after each harvest's breeding phase, automatically gain 1 food per unused spot.
Card itself holds up to 3 same-type animals (a new slot, not a pasture); always-on.
Schedules a stone-room PIECE (not goods) onto round space 12; at start of round 12, if living in a stone house (state check), optional free build-room sub-action, else the piece is discarded.

Immediately after using a wood accumulation space (T-AFTER), optionally build exactly 1 new pasture (subdivision excluded) paying only with the just-taken wood (needs provenance of that wood); one fence free.

cards.new_category: SPACE-DEPOSIT: place goods paid for an effect back onto an action space's accumulation pile for future visitors
cards.note: Each use of a wood accumulation space (default T-BEFORE), optionally pay 2 wood to plow 1 field; the paid wood is deposited onto the space itself — no code covers depositing goods on an action space.
Schedules a field TILE (piece, not goods) onto round space current+building-resources; at that round's start, optional plow of the field.
Schedules field TILES onto round spaces current+7/8/9; at each round's start, optional plow of that field.

4-food stack on card; each grain-obtaining event (once per event regardless of amount) auto-pays 1 food from it; when the stack empties (threshold), grants an optional Family Growth even without room.

Errata'd: play in work phase; +2 food, mandatory immediate extra placement of a supply person (a temporary worker), who is mandatorily removed from play at the next returning-home phase (needs a pending-removal flag); can never grow to 5 people.

Reveals the hidden round-14 action-space card early and hosts it in front of the owner as an action space usable exclusively by them until round 14 — early-reveal + exclusive-use is a one-off mechanic.
Each use of Grain Utilization (T-BEFORE default), optionally replace one of its two actions with a full Build Fences action.

Fires at end of each own turn in which a room/stable/fence was built (needs a within-turn built-something counter; off-turn builds excluded per clarification); optionally grants building one of 3 specific majors at normal cost.
After using Forest: optionally return 2 wood ONTO that accumulation space (cost destination is the board space, not the supply) to play an occupation with its play-cost waived.
Compound same-turn condition (used a Lessons space AND a cooking improvement) — needs per-turn tracking of both events; awards 1 bonus point automatically when both occur.
During fence building, may substitute 2 wood for a fence piece on perimeter edges only; palisade edges function as fences (can exceed 15), each worth 1 bonus point — needs per-edge palisade state.
Pure end-game scoring rule: 2 bonus points if >=2 orthogonally adjacent unused farm spaces exist (adjacency check = farm geometry); the -1s still apply.
At any time, exchange 1/3/5 grain for 3/4/5 food plus 0/1/2 bonus points; bonus points earned in-play need a running counter.

One-shot armed trigger: the NEXT time animals are taken from an accumulation space AND all are accommodated, 1 bonus point per animal; a take without full accommodation neither fires nor voids (armed flag + payload of how many animals were taken/placed).
Once per game, fires the moment the sheep count on the farm reaches the player-count-dependent threshold (7 sheep at 2-3 players): +2 bonus points.
Prereq is just a state check; effect is 1 food on play — trivially implementable.

cards.new_category: E-RESTRICT: a continuous NEGATIVE legality rule that forbids the owner from using otherwise-legal farm spaces until a condition lifts
cards.note: Blocks using unused spaces orthogonally adjacent to the house until all other spaces are used (with the same-action exception per clarification) — a self-restriction no effect code captures.
'Each time you use' Fishing or Reed Bank → before-window automatic bonus of 1 grain + 1 wood.
On play, schedules 2 wood / 1 boar alternating on the next 4 round spaces (player chooses the starting good — an on-play mandatory choice); the boars must later be accommodated.

The card is a shared action space offering a wood-for-wood+food exchange (5/7/9 wood -> 8 wood + 2/4/7 food, optional within the action); other players using it must first pay the owner 1 food (mandatory opponent-action toll).
Each use of the Grain Seed / Vegetable Seed space places 1 food on each of the next 3 (grain) / 2 (veg) round spaces, collected at those rounds' starts.
On play, schedules 2 food on round spaces current+3 and current+4; cost is a wood-or-clay choice.
On play, schedules 1 food on next 4 round spaces + 1 stone on the 5th; cost is 3 wood or 2 clay.
Seeds a food pile on the card; wood-accum use moves 1 food to supply, stone-accum use adds 2 to the pile — both mandatory 'each time you use' (before) hooks; alt play cost.

Fires after you place any improvement/occupation in front of you; conditional plain state read (equal counts) then automatic 2 food; clarification defers the check until a chained play fully resolves.
Each clay-accumulation-space use gives food equal to boar count; round-7+ is a play restriction, no tag.
Play condition is a plain state read (pasture spaces >= completed rounds); one-time food equal to current round — trivial.
Optional end-of-round (non-harvest rounds) exchange: 1 wood->2 food or 1 stone->4 food.
2 food on play; after each unconditional Sow action, 1 food per stable — automatic sub-action after-hook.
3-food pile on card; wood-accum use pays 1 from the pile (space hook), each renovate restocks to 3 (renovate sub-action hook).
1 food on play; during harvest field phase, +1 food per vegetable harvested from fields — automatic.
One-time food gain whose amount (4 vs 2) depends on WHICH action space the card was played from — needs the play-context provenance.
Optional on each Fishing use: pay 1 grain to schedule 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces.
On each Lessons-space use, before paying the occupation cost, optionally exchange 1 grain for 4 food.
Each Grain Utilization use gives +2 food if Fishing is occupied — reading a named space's occupancy is a plain state read, not board geometry.
Play-cost fork (wood/clay/stone) determines the reward size (2/3/4 round spaces of grain) -> E-PLAYVARIANT; grain placed on future round spaces -> E-SCHED.
Fires before each Bake Bread sub-action (mandatory per clarification: must bake if grain received -> F-AUTO); grain count needs to know which spaces your workers occupy -> ST-PLACELOG.
'At any time' -> ATWILL; cost includes 2 food -> E-FOODCOST; vegetables placed on the next 2 round spaces -> E-SCHED.
Optional buy hooked before the start of specific rounds (rounds ending with a harvest are fixed, not geometry); food-for-crop purchase = conversion costing food.
One-time on-play goods gain conditional on a plain state read (harvests completed == occupations played) -> trivially implementable.

Always-on rule change: extends sow legality to small pastures (which then also count as fields), and overrides/reduces those pastures' animal capacity while used this way. Pasture-size condition is a count, not adjacency, so no L-GEOMFARM.
One-time on-play goods keyed on a plain room-count read; prereq (3 Occupations) is not a mechanic.
Fires before you play (minor) or build (major) an improvement; wood arrives before payment so it can fund the improvement itself (the clarification) -> T-BEFORE; automatic gain -> F-AUTO.
On play schedules 1 wood on next 5 rounds (E-SCHED); a one-shot hook on your next renovate sub-action mandatorily removes the still-promised wood (E-TAKEBACK, fires once -> CAP-GAME).
Places reed on fixed-numbered future round spaces (rounds 5/8/10/12 are fixed every game, not board geometry).

Fires on any player (both actors) taking >=3 stone from an accumulation space; conditioned on the amount actually taken -> T-AFTER + needs the taken-amount payload (ST-PROV); automatic gain from general supply.

Optional, fires before each work phase (start-of-round seam); takes 1 resource off a sufficiently-full accumulation space (reduces the board pile - nearest code is E-GOODS; thresholds 6/5/4/4 are a plain board read).

Optional food-for-resource buy after each harvest completes; no exact 'harvest end' seam exists, so tagged as after the breeding phase (the harvest's final phase). Once per harvest by nature, so no CAP tag.
On play builds an ordered pile of goods ON the card (ST-STACK), consumed top-down at will for 1 clay each (a clay-for-good conversion); pile includes animals needing accommodation -> E-ANIMALS.
On play grants a build-1-stable sub-action (the stable itself free; card cost 1 wood is normal); then passes to another player.
On play (work-phase condition is a plain state check) you may immediately place an additional person; passing card.

Places stone onto empty fields as if planted, harvested normally in field phases (errata) — a field-content/field-yield manipulation; 1-food play cost; passing card. Stone arrives via field-phase harvest, not immediately.
The card itself is a new animal holder (1 sheep + 1 boar + 1 cattle); always-on, not a pasture.
Continuous cost change: reed component removed from renovation and room-build costs.
On play grants a full (optional) Build Fences action with grant-scoped free fences on edges adjacent to field tiles (farm adjacency); 2-food play cost.
4 field tiles stacked on the card; each own use of Farmland (default T-BEFORE) optionally grants plowing up to 2 fields from the card's pool.
Fires immediately after your last action-space use of a work phase if it was an accumulation space (last-ness computable from persons left); grants an optional Bake Bread sub-action.
On play +2 food; each own use of Farmland or Cultivation optionally grants a Bake Bread sub-action ('each time you use' = T-BEFORE).
Continuous: extends eligibility (build 3 named majors via a Minor Improvement action) and reduces their cost by 1 stone.
End-game scoring rule (+1/stone room), mutually exclusive with other stone-house bonus cards; no hook — scoring is computed.

cards.new_category: L-FORBID: a continuous rule that FORBIDS an otherwise-legal option (here: discarding hand cards unplayed)
cards.note: Negative scoring rule (-1 per hand card) plus a standing prohibition on discarding hand cards; the play restriction ('not if you already discarded') is a state-readable prereq needing an already-discarded flag only if discarding exists.
Self-imposed continuous cost change: every new field tile placement costs 1 food (must be payable before plowing — affects plow legality); food cost may need conversion.
After each harvest's feeding phase, optional exchange of exactly 1 food for 2 different building resources or 1 vegetable; per-harvest by nature (no CAP tag).
Fires immediately after own use of Vegetable Seeds (T-AFTER); optional extra worker placement restricted to accumulation spaces, plus +1 good of the accumulating type.
On play, schedules 1 food on each of the next 8 round spaces; play cost is 2 Wood OR 2 Clay (plus 1 reed) = alt cost.

cards.new_category: ACCUMSEED: the card places goods onto a main-board accumulation action space (not a future round space)
cards.note: Two hooks: opponent using Fishing must first pay owner 1 food (T-BEFORE, A-OPP, mandatory — also restricts opponents' legality: need 1 non-Fishing food to place there); then at that round's returning-home, 2 food is added to the Fishing space only if it was used (ST-STORE flag; ST-PROV for the no-Fishing-food payment restriction).
Auto food per stable built (3 for the last/4th — read from stable count); plus a passive cost mod: per build-stables action, exactly one stable may be paid with 1 clay instead of wood.
Optional feeding-phase conversion (exactly 1 veg -> 5 food, once per feeding by nature — no CAP tag); plus a plain end-game bonus rule (1 pt each for 5th/6th vegetable).
Schedules 1 grain on rounds 8/10/12 (fixed round numbers, not board geometry); play cost 3 Wood OR 3 Clay.
Each time after you play an occupation, gain 1 vegetable — mandatory own-play card hook.
Card is itself a veg-only field (passive rule + L-CARDFIELD); after harvesting from this card you can immediately convert each such veg to 4 food (needs provenance: veg from this card).
Schedules 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space — fixed round numbers, plain scheduling.
Schedules 1 stone on each remaining even-numbered round space; the '2 Occupations' prereq gets no code.
One-shot food on play, amount read from the current round number (a plain state read); round-restriction is a prerequisite; passing card.
On play gain 1 boar + 1 cattle (must be accommodated); 2-sheep cost is an ordinary animal cost, no code; passing card.

Optional returning-home-phase hook (once per round by nature): if rooms > people, pay 1 food (food cost, convertible) for a granted Family Growth sub-action — requires a room (rooms > people condition).
Auto +1 food in each feeding phase (usable for that feeding -> before feeding resolves); plus a passive capacity override: house can no longer hold animals, even via other cards.
Always-available replacement cost formula (2 clay+1 wood+1 reed instead of 5 clay+2 reed) whenever building a clay room — a continuous cost modifier, no firing event.
On play stocks 2 field tiles on the card (ST-STACK); each use of Farmland/Cultivation optionally grants extra plows consuming tiles from the card. 'Each time you use' = T-BEFORE default.
On using Lessons, optionally grants an additional play-occupation sub-action at a fixed 2-food occupation cost (food cost → E-FOODCOST).
End-game bonus rule (no hook); depends on how animals are assigned to unfenced stables (exactly 1 of a type per stable), so scoring requires optimizing the animal arrangement.
Only a state-computable play restriction (rounds left vs unused farmyard spaces) plus its printed VP — per ruling, a prerequisite/condition needs no mechanic → NONE.
Mandatory food income in each harvest field phase scaled by cattle count (1/3/5 → 1/2/3), plus a passive end-game bonus rule (1 pt per 2 cattle).
Optional pay-1-food-for-1-bonus-point on each use of a wood accumulation space (needs ≥1 boar); bonus points earned through actions accumulate → running counter.
On play schedules alternating food and sheep on all remaining round spaces; the sheep arriving must be accommodated → E-ANIMALS.

Fires after each of your occupation plays, granting a good keyed to the running occupation count; the 6th reward is a field (a plow → E-GRANTSUB). 'Not retroactively' needs a snapshot of occupations already played → ST-STORE.
Pure on-play scheduling of 0/1/2/3 food onto the next 4 round spaces.
On-play scheduling of 1 food on each of the next N round spaces, N = current sheep count (a plain state read at play time).
Mandatory 3 food immediately before each own occupation play (explicitly before paying the occupation cost).

cards.new_category: L-RESTRICT: a permanent rule change that FORBIDS the player from using certain action spaces (the inverse of L-EXT, which extends legality)
cards.note: One-time 6 food on play, plus a permanent negative legality rule (rounds 12–14 action spaces unusable for you) that no existing legality code captures.
Optional 1-clay→1-grain exchange before each Bake Bread sub-action; clarification makes the bake mandatory once the exchange is taken (the option is on the exchange, not the bake).

On play schedules 1 veg on rounds +4/+7; at the start of those rounds the veg is not collected free — an optional purchase for 1 food (F-TRIG + E-FOODCOST). T-BEFORE as default for a start-of-round seam.
'Each time after you use Fishing' = T-AFTER on that space; optional buy of grain (1 food) or veg (3 food) — a food-for-crop exchange.

cards.new_category: RESOURCE-CROP: a building resource (wood) is sowable/harvestable as if it were a crop
cards.note: The card itself is a field (counts as 2 fields for sowing, 1 for scoring) that only wood may be planted on; wood stacks live on the card and yield in the field phase like grain. Errata restricts to wood only.
Pure scheduling: 1 reed on each of the next 3 round spaces, collected automatically at the start of those rounds.
Fires automatically at the end of fixed rounds 8/10/12 (fixed round numbers, not board geometry); payout is a plain state read (veg fields, veg in supply).
On play +1 sheep (must be accommodated). Each harvest feeding phase, optional exchange of exactly 1 veg for 1 animal of an owned type (also E-ANIMALS — the animal needs accommodation).

cards.new_category: FENCE-REARRANGE: raze all existing fences and rebuild the whole layout in a new configuration (animals kept)
cards.note: One-shot full-refence: remove all built fences, rebuild them plus up to 3 more from supply at no wood cost; clarification says this counts as building fences (fires fence-build combos). Fence pieces from supply, not resources. Passes after resolving.
Immediate wood equal to own occupation count (plain state read), then passes.
Immediate clay per clay room + per major improvement (plain state read), then passes.
Immediate food with a mandatory choice: exactly 3 food OR 1 food per sheep (sheep kept). Choice is at play time, not a hook, so no F-MANDCHOICE.

Optionally adds 1 matching good directly onto each field holding exactly 1 good — goods go onto fields, not supply (crop manipulation, not E-GOODS). Clarification: excludes Mud Patch boar; interacts with Wood Field stacks.
Optional immediate purchase of 1 sheep/boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food, gated on being able to accommodate it; food cost may need conversion.

Continuous capacity rule conditional on a standing state read (>=4 rooms → house holds 5 people). Not LATCH: the effect is a persistent capacity change, not a one-shot firing; the condition is computable from state each time.
The card itself is an animal holder for cattle with dynamic capacity = current pasture count; holds but does not provide animals (no E-ANIMALS).

'On your first renovation' = ACTION trigger on the renovate sub-action + CAP-GAME (not LATCH); optional ('you can'); L-EXT for the new wood-to-stone-directly renovation target, E-COSTMOD for the 2-stone discount.
'Each time before you use Pig Market' = before-space hook granting an optional full Build Fences action; wood paid as usual (no cost mod).
On play, optional plow of 1 field ('you can'); L-EXT because the plowed field is exempt from the adjacency rule.
'At any time' = ATWILL; cost is discarding all goods from a planted field (E-CROPMANIP) to gain a plow sub-action; repeatable, no cap stated.
'Each time you use Farmland/Cultivation' = before-space hook; grants one optional additional plow sub-action.
Fires each time a Quarry accumulation space is revealed (nature event; A-OWN as owner's option); optional Family Growth without placing a person, room-in-house required (normal growth, needs room).

On play, optionally move the FIRST person placed this work phase onto the card (needs the placement log) and then place another person; clarification requires knowing whether that placement added a newborn (event provenance).

Standing rule letting one person use Day Laborer + the ADJACENT Lessons space (two spaces with one person = E-WORKERMANIP; space adjacency = L-GEOMBOARD); both must be unoccupied, both become occupied. Activation modeled as PASSIVE since it modifies the normal placement rather than firing on an event.
One-shot hook at the start of the NEXT harvest only (needs a flag for which harvest); optional Family Growth requiring room for the newborn; newborn must be fed.
'Each time you use Major Improvement / House Redevelopment' = before-space hook; grants an optional play-occupation sub-action costing 1 food (E-FOODCOST).

'At the end of the field phase of each harvest' = T-AFTER on the harvest field phase; optional pay-1-grain for 2 bonus points (bonus points via an action legitimately combine HOOK + E-SCORE; needs a running bonus counter) and each other player gets 1 food (E-OPPTRANSFER).
Pure end-game scoring rule (bonus = own negative points, capped at 7) computed at scoring; no hook. Depends only on own score, not comparative.
The 5-cards restriction is a play prerequisite computable from state (per ruling: no mechanic); card otherwise has no stated effect beyond its play — NONE.
End-game tiered bonus for a plain count of unplanted fields — ordinary E-SCORE (threshold count, no optimization); clarifications only refine what counts as unplanted.

cards.new_category: S-HEND: fires at the end of a harvest (after the breeding phase completes)
cards.note: Optional after-each-harvest exchange (1 reed -> 1 bonus point); no listed seam for 'end of harvest', nearest is S-HBREED; earned bonus points accumulate on the card.

cards.new_category: GOODS-TO-BOARD: a good paid by the player is placed onto an action space's accumulation pile (rejoins the board supply)
cards.note: Fires immediately after using any clay accumulation space; optional exchange of 1 clay for a bonus point, with the spent clay placed back on that space.
One-shot on play: every other player gains 1 food (goods flowing to opponents; from general supply, not the owner).
Mandatory +1 bonus point each use of Traveling Players; in 1-3p (incl. 2p) the card itself becomes a Traveling-Players accumulation space (Fishing effect) usable by all players -> L-CARDSPACE.
Play cost forks with the reward: pay 1 grain -> 1 vegetable, or 1 reed -> 4 wood.
Triggers on the build-major-improvement sub-action (any entry point, not just the Maj/Min space); schedules 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces.
Each use of Day Laborer automatically schedules 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces.
Once per round, optionally pay 1 vegetable for 1 bonus point plus food scheduled on the next 2 round spaces; bonus points accumulate.
One-shot on play: place 1 food on remaining round spaces, count capped at 3x planted fields (plain state read).
End-of-turn check: needs a per-turn flag recording whether an unused farmyard space became used this turn; +1 food if so.
During each feeding phase, optional 1 grain -> 5 food conversion, once per empty unfenced stable (plain state count gates uses).
On play: food equal to completed rounds left (state read); plus an unlimited at-will exchange 1 sheep + 1 boar -> 1 cattle (provides an animal needing accommodation).
Obtain-hook restricted by provenance: only wild boar gained from an action space's effect; +1 food per such boar.

cards.new_category: S-CONVERTUSE: fires on using a (cooking-improvement) goods-to-food conversion, keyed on the conversion's batch size
cards.note: Triggers whenever a cooking improvement converts 2+ goods simultaneously (+1 food); no listed seam covers conversion-use events, and it needs the conversion payload (batch size).
Play cost is a stable piece (E-PIECECOST) and each use pays a fence piece from supply; optional exchange (grain+fence -> 5 food) fires after each harvest's field phase.
Optional per-harvest conversion (1 wood/clay/stone -> 2/2/3 food) usable during the feeding phase; once per harvest is inherent to the seam.
On play, optional buy of 1 veg for 3 food (food cost); at any time, discard 1 planted veg from a field stacked >=2 (crop manipulation) for 4 food.
Fires immediately after using any wood accumulation space (per clarification, before other after-triggers); mandatory 1 food if supply <=5 wood — plain state check.

Play requires returning the built Clay/Stone Oven (E-RETURNCOMP); on play +5 food; before each Bake Bread you may build the Clay/Stone Oven major (tagged E-GRANTACT — grants a major-improvement build, not a listed primitive). Clarification: counts as an oven for card references but cannot itself bake.
On each Bake Bread action (must bake normally), optional once-per-action 1 grain -> 2 food + 1 bonus point; bonus points accumulate over the game (ST-COUNTER).
Each harvest, each owned cooking improvement can be used once at doubled food rate for 1 animal/veg — needs a per-improvement per-harvest used flag (ST-STORE).
Feeding-phase optional exchange: 1 supply grain + 1 grain removed from a field (crop manipulation) -> 4 food + 2 bonus points (accumulated).
Once per round, pay 1 grain (ordinary cost) to schedule 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces.
Fires after each whole harvest (tagged as after the breeding phase — no end-of-harvest seam exists); mandatory: 2 grain in supply -> 1 food, 3+ -> 1 grain (errata: or, not and).
Start-of-round auto grain if >=1 sheep in a pasture, only in non-harvest rounds (fixed round schedule, plain state check); before/after timing is degenerate at the SOR seam.
At any time, swap 3 planted grain in a field for 1 vegetable placed in that same field — pure on-field crop manipulation, no loose goods gained.
After gaining newborns of >=2 types in a breeding phase (clarification: only accommodated newborns count), grants a Sow sub-action — granted sub-actions are optional (F-TRIG).
1-food play cost; on play, carries out an off-schedule field-phase harvest for you only (an extra field-harvest, tagged E-CROPMANIP + E-GOODS), then grants a full Major-or-Minor-Improvement action.

Fires after each own Sow sub-action; mandatory 1 grain (F-AUTO) that from round 11 becomes a mandatory grain-OR-vegetable choice (F-MANDCHOICE); round number is plain public state. 'Unconditional sow' is a trigger condition, no extra mechanic.

Two hooks: an automatic returning-home accrual of wood ONTO the card (S-ROUNDEND, F-AUTO, ST-STACK), and an optional before-build hook (T-BEFORE, F-TRIG) when building a Fireplace/Cooking Hearth/oven (improvement build — nearest seam S-MAJMIN) that moves up to 4 stacked wood to supply (E-GOODS).
'Each time you use a wood accumulation space' = before-use space hook, mandatory +2 wood.
On play, places 1 clay on each of the next 3 round spaces, collected at round start — pure scheduled-goods mechanic.
On play, schedules 1 reed on rounds current+5/7/9/11/13 — fixed offsets from the current round, scheduled goods, not board geometry.
Optional buy (1 food -> 1 stone) alongside each own plow sub-action; clarification extends the trigger to playing field cards (S-PLAY). The buy pairs with, not modifies, the plow — tagged T-AFTER.

On play stocks 2 of each resource onto the card (ST-STACK); then a mandatory hook when ANY player obtains >= a per-resource threshold — needs which resource and how many were taken (ST-PROV); owner gains 1 of that resource from the card.
'Each time after you use Day Laborer' — after-space hook, optional buy of 1 of each building resource for 2 food (food cost payable by conversion).
One-shot on-play gain of 2 cattle, which must be accommodated; the 1-pasture prereq is just a HAVE-check.

Once per non-harvest-ending round, optionally pay 1 grain to breed 1 animal type outside a breeding phase (breed-rule change + newborn to accommodate). Read as an at-will once-per-round option; exact in-round timing not stated. Grain is a plain resource cost — E-FOODCOST omitted... correction: cost is grain, not food, so no E-FOODCOST.
Passing minor granting an optional immediate plow constrained by farm-tile shape (the plowed field must complete a zigzag pattern with existing fields).
Passing minor granting an optional immediate Renovation (renovate primitive sub-action, paid at normal cost).
Passing minor granting an optional immediate sow limited to exactly 1 field.
Passing minor: immediate wood equal to current stone count — a plain state read plus the passing mechanic.
Passing minor with 1-food play cost; on play gains 1 clay per planted field (plain state read).
Passing minor; one-time optional exchange of up to 3 wood at 1 wood -> 1 stone on play.
Gains 2 food + 1 grain on play; the grain MUST be sown immediately — a mandatory (not optional) granted sow sub-action; then passes.
Always-on capacity increase for size-1 pastures (3 instead of 2; 6 with stable).
At-any-time renovate to stone with a replacement cost formula (per-room, includes food from wood); wood->stone directly is a new renovation target (L-EXT).
Fires immediately before a renovate-to-stone sub-action: 2 clay + 1 reed automatic (F-AUTO), plus an optional whole Build Rooms action (F-TRIG).
Each use of Sheep/Cattle Market fires before the space's action; optional single-stable build with a grant-specific cost (1 wood at Sheep Market, free at Cattle Market). Play cost includes 1 food.
Before each unconditional Sow sub-action, optionally pay 3 food (food cost, convertible) to plow 1 field.
After each returning-home phase, optionally pay 2 wood + 1 food to take the Farmland space's action without placing a person.

cards.new_category: PAY-TO-BOARD: a cost is paid onto an action/accumulation space (the good joins that space's accumulated stock) rather than returning to the general supply
cards.note: After using any clay accumulation space, optionally pay 1 clay to plow 1 field — the paid clay is placed back on that accumulation space, not the supply.

Each time you GET a Minor Improvement action (any entry point, not just the Maj/Min space — S-MAJMIN is the nearest seam), you may take Family Growth instead (needs room). Errata: only from round 5 on (plain state read). Play cost 1 food.

cards.new_category: SCHED-PERSON: place a PERSON (not goods/animals) on a computed future round space, becoming a usable worker when that round arrives
cards.note: On play, computes round = current + building-resource count and places a supply person there; text says only 'in that round, you can use the person' — ambiguous whether the person persists afterward (noted, but mechanics tagged either way).

The card is a personal action space; rounds 3-5 it grants a renovation sub-action, rounds 6-8 a mandatory choice of 1 veg / boar / cattle (animals must be accommodated). Round bands are fixed, not board geometry.

While at exactly 4 people (state read), placement-rule change: 3rd and 4th person may be placed back-to-back in one turn (two placements in a turn) and even on the SAME space (second placement on an occupied space). Play cost 1 food.

cards.new_category: IDENTITY-FLEX: the card counts as one of several entity types (field / occupation / minor improvement / major improvement) — whichever is most convenient for any other effect, but never more than one at once.
cards.note: Permanent multi-identity card: sowable field on the card (L-CARDFIELD), counts as the Fireplace major (errata strips its conversions, so mainly identity/VP/upgrade eligibility -> L-EXT) and as an occupation/minor/major for other effects; play-via-Minor-action is just a play restriction.
Always-on legality extension: a Minor Improvement action may instead build the Joinery/Well majors, and one Major Improvement action may build both — substitutes/extends what those actions can build.
Fires at the end of any turn in which you renovated (renovate sub-action, after-timing), optional; returns your Joinery/Pottery improvement in exchange for the next-tier major from supply.
Pure end-game bonus rule: 2/5/8 pts for owning 3/4/5 bottom-row majors — a plain count, no hook.
End-game bonus: points per grain+vegetable pair counting supply and fields — plain computed score.
Scoring reads a value from a PAST moment (goods in fields before the final harvest), so it needs a snapshot stored at final-harvest start; the scoring rule itself is a plain 2-pt threshold.
End-game bonus per unused space orthogonally adjacent to the house (stone house condition) — farm-adjacency geometry; the wooden-house prereq is untagged.
End-game 4-pt bonus if stone rooms > people; explicitly mutually exclusive with other stone-house bonus cards (E-SCOREGRP).
End-game bonus: 1 pt per Nth animal, divisor set by player count (fixed at setup) — plain count score.
End-game 3-pt bonus, but requires game-long tracking: a running count of sheep gained from NON-breeding sources (needs provenance of each gain) plus a flag for ever cooking a sheep.
On play, alternately schedules 1 clay / 1 wild boar onto all remaining round spaces; the boars are animals that must be accommodated when collected.
On play, places 1 food on each remaining round space up to current wood count — a plain state read gating a scheduled-goods placement.
Once per round, optionally pay 1 reed (ordinary resource cost, no code) to place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces.
On play, places 2 food on every remaining round space; the 10-cards prereq is a play condition, no mechanic.
Fires before each round starts; automatic 1 food conditional on a plain hand-count comparison (occupations vs improvements in hand).

The card is a public action space (1 food automatic on use, any player); immediately before returning home the placed person may optionally be reused on an unoccupied action space (worker reuse, needs to track the worker on this card).
Automatic 1 food in each returning-home phase, conditional on a plain supply comparison (clay > wood).

cards.new_category: TURN-DEFER: skip a scheduled worker placement now (taking a substitute benefit) and make that placement later in the same round
cards.note: Once per round the player may replace placing their second person with 1 food; the skipped person can still be placed later that round, which needs turn-order/deferred-placement handling no code covers.
Start of each work phase, automatic 1 food conditional on the food count on the named Fishing space (a plain state read of a named space, not board geometry).
'Each time you use' a round-space card in the fixed 8-11 band (fixed round numbers, not geometry) → +1 food automatically.

cards.new_category: S-CONVERT: a hook seam keyed on converting a good into food via a (cooking) improvement — no listed seam covers conversion events
cards.note: Fires on your own sheep/boar→food cooking conversion (+1 food, automatic); needs the conversion's payload (which animal, via a cooking improvement).
One-time on play: schedules 1 food on every remaining round space, collected at the start of those rounds.
Fires at the end of any action whose sow included vegetables (needs the sow payload), then pays 1 food per vegetable field owned.

cards.new_category: CARD-IDENTITY: the card counts as either 1 minor or 1 major improvement, whichever is convenient for other effects (never both at once)
cards.note: Played by returning a Fireplace; grants at-will veg/animal→food conversions plus a Bake Bread rate (grain→2 food); clarification adds a flexible minor/major identity rule.
Played by returning the Pottery; at-will clay→2 food conversion; end-game bonus points from a plain clay-count threshold table (E-SCORE, not fired).
Start of each harvest, automatic 2 food conditional on a plain count of grain fields (including planted field cards).
2 food immediately on play; plus an optional feeding-phase harvest conversion (2/3/4 grain → 3/6/9 food) each harvest.
Harvest field phase: 1 food per field tile that was harvested this phase (needs which fields yielded) and is orthogonally adjacent to the house (farm adjacency).
End-of-round hook (rounds not ending in a harvest, computable) granting an optional Bake Bread; the card itself supplies the Grain→2 Food bake rate, so it also acts as a baking-rate source.
Fires in each harvest field phase, mandatory; needs the field-phase yield payload (how much grain was harvested) to test the >=2 condition, then grants 1 grain.
On play, schedules 1 grain onto the next 3 of the fixed harvest-round spaces (4/7/9/11/13/14) — fixed round numbers, not board geometry.
Optional pay-1-reed-for-1-veg exchange each time you use Reed Bank ('each time you use' = before). The 4+ player reed-return clause is inert in 2p.
Optional hook before each harvest's field phase; pays 1 grain (ordinary cost) to add vegetables onto up to 2 planted veg fields — crops-on-fields manipulation.

'At any time' deferrable: discard a field's last crop and take a Sow limited to that field. The 'exactly 1 good AS A RESULT OF A HARVEST' condition needs a per-field flag distinguishing harvest-depleted fields from other 1-good states (e.g. cards that add crops), hence ST-STORE.
Optional field-phase hook that changes how many goods fields yield (+1 good from up to N fields, N = unfenced stables); count is a plain state read.

End-of-turn rebate: needs a running count of wood actually paid during the turn's Farm Expansion / improvement builds (payment provenance accumulated over the turn), then pays back floor(paid/2). Play cost is 1 food.
Immediately after each harvest feeding phase, if food supply is 0 (plain state read), automatically gain 1 wood + 1 clay.
Fires on ANY player's renovate-to-stone sub-action (both actors); mandatory clay gain scaled by that player's room count — readable from state at fire time.

After you build an improvement, if it cost >=2 clay, gain 1 stone — needs the payment payload (clay actually paid, since cost modifiers can change it). Play cost is 1 Wood OR 1 Food (alt-cost, food branch).
Every renovate: −1 reed on the cost (before/during payment) and +1 stone at the end of the action (after) — one hook with both a before cost-modifier and an after reward.

Passive cost modifiers scoped to two action spaces (improvements via House Redevelopment −1 resource of choice; fences via Farm Redevelopment −3 wood total = free-fence budget). Play cost forks: return 1 boar, or cook it (yielding food at cooking rate) — cost/reward variant. Clarification: Merchant-played improvements not discounted.
Each use of the Fencing space automatically also yields 1 wild boar (must be accommodated). Play cost is 2 Food OR 1 Grain (alt-cost; the food branch may need conversion).
On play grants up to 3 free build-stable sub-actions (granted subs optional); zero-cost is a grant parameter, not a lasting cost modifier; passing minor.
On play grants a free renovate-to-clay sub-action (optional); prereq wooden house is just a HAVE-check; passing minor.
On play returns your placed worker from Grain Utilization home for reuse this round (needs placement knowledge); 1-food play cost; passing minor.
On play removes planted grain from one field (crop manipulation) and pays 2 wood per grain removed; passing minor.

cards.new_category: ACC-DRAIN: takes goods directly off the board's accumulation action spaces into your supply (denying them to opponents)
cards.note: On play takes 2 different building resources FROM accumulation spaces (a board-drain, not general supply); 2-food play cost; passing minor.
Plain conditional goods gain from a state read (1 of each resource type you hold 4+ of); passing minor is its only non-trivial mechanic.
Trivial: pay 1 grain (ordinary cost), gain 4 food on play; passing minor.
On play gain 1 vegetable; play cost is 2 food (convertible-food cost); passing minor.
On play, up to two immediate conversions of any 2/3/4 building resources into 1 sheep/boar/cattle — animals gained must be accommodated; passing minor.

Fires at end of work phases of rounds 3 & 6 (fixed rounds, not board geometry): optionally move your Farmland worker to an unoccupied space and take that action, or take 1 food; needs to know the worker is on Farmland.
Always-on: the card itself holds animals (any type) up to room count, conditional on a pasture orthogonally adjacent to the house (farm geometry).
Always-on capacity increase to existing slots (+1 per unfenced stable, +2 per pasture-with-stable); counting stables/pastures is not geometry.
'At any time' renovate clay→stone without placing a person, paying the normal renovation cost — an at-will granted renovate sub-action.

Condition-gated at-will: whenever all other players have more people than you, you may take a full Build Rooms action without placing a person; 'each time' frequency (once per condition instance vs. while it holds) is a minor ruling to confirm.

cards.new_category: SPACE-DEPOSIT: a cost paid by placing a good from your supply onto an action space, where it joins the space's accumulated goods for the next visitor
cards.note: Fires after using any wood accumulation space; optional; grants a full Build Fences action. The 1-stone cost is placed ON the space for the next visitor, which no code captures.
Always-on: fence edges on the farmyard-board perimeter cost no wood (fence pieces still used). Positional/edge geometry of the farm, hence L-GEOMFARM.

Two parts: optional extra plow (2 instead of 1) when using Farmland/Cultivation (hook on the space, before), plus a mandatory always-on sow rule change (1 fewer good per sown field) = passive crop-on-field rule modification.
After each subsequent minor-improvement play (own), optionally pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
After using Cattle Market, optionally pay 2 food (convertible) to plow 1 field.
End of each work phase, if your people occupy both Grain Seeds and Vegetable Seeds (needs worker-placement log), optional plow of 1 field.
Always-on legality change: may use any Wish for Children space even when occupied by another player.

On play, load any food from supply onto the card (stack). Once per round, discard 1 food FROM THE CARD (not convertible supply food, so not E-FOODCOST) to place a person from your supply that round — a temporary/extra worker placement.
End of each work phase: optional constrained sow (exactly 1 crop on 1 field).

Whenever an action space gives you a Minor Improvement action (any such space, not only Major/Minor Improvement), you may build a major improvement instead — action substitution, optional, decided before resolving.

cards.new_category: FIELDPHASE-NOW: run the harvest FIELD phase out of schedule, for one player only
cards.note: On play, immediately execute the harvest field phase on your farm only — harvests goods off your fields (gain crops); an out-of-schedule invocation of the field-harvest rule.
End of work phase in rounds 7 and 9 (fixed round numbers, not board geometry): optional Sow action without placing a person.

Two mechanics: an end-of-work-phase hook to bank 1 food onto the card (irretrievable stack), and an at-will discard of 6 card-food to build a major improvement at no cost (cost paid from the on-card stack, not convertible supply food).

On play, mark round current+3 (stored flag). At the start of that round (SOR hook; timing tagged T-BEFORE = before the round's work — a round-start event has no natural before/after), optionally play 1 occupation with its occupation cost waived.
No effect beyond playing it; prereq ('your person on Day Laborer') is a HAVE-check per rules, though note the engine must be able to read current worker placement to check it.

cards.new_category: FEEDMOD: changes how much food a person/newborn requires during the feeding phase
cards.note: Alt play-cost (1 wood OR 1 clay); continuous negative rule making newborns eat 2 food each feeding — a feeding-requirement modifier, nearest code is E-PEOPLE (newborn classification).

Fires on each subsequent improvement built/played (majors + minors — S-PLAY covers both); optional pay-1-reed for 1 bonus point, capped cumulatively at room count (ST-COUNTER tracks reed placed). Timing read as after the improvement is completed.
Pure end-game scoring rule over the farmyard's 5 columns — positional/shape read of room placement, hence L-GEOMFARM.

Continuous: one designated pasture-with-stable loses all animal capacity (ST-STORE records which). Hook: each time you obtain reed FROM AN ACTION SPACE (source-restricted S-OBTAIN), automatically +1 bonus point (accumulated → ST-COUNTER).
Plain coverage-threshold end-game score (no unused farm spaces → 2 pts); counting, not geometry.
Plain end-game score keyed on exact people count (4/3/2 → 2/3/5 pts).
Continuous constraint: at least one pasture must stay empty — effectively removes usable animal capacity / restricts accommodation legality.
On play +1 food; plus a plain end-game bonus rule (3 pts if no cattle at scoring).
Each Fishing use (default T-BEFORE), optionally place up to 2 wood on the card; scoring counts accumulated wood with the 1st/4th/7th/10th worth 0 — needs a running per-card count.
'Each harvest, you can' = optional once-per-harvest exchange (1 clay → choice of 2 food or 1 bonus point); bonus points earned during play need accumulation.
On play stacks 5 goods on the card in fixed order; each Day Laborer use (default T-BEFORE) automatically yields the top good until the pile is consumed.
On play schedules alternating food/clay onto the remaining even-numbered round spaces (fixed round numbers — not board geometry), collected at those rounds' starts.
On play schedules cattle/grain/cattle onto the next 3 round spaces; the cattle must later be accommodated on the farm.
On play, reads stable count (plain state) and places 1 food on each of the next 2-5 round spaces; collected at round start.
On play, places 1 food on each remaining odd-numbered round space (fixed numbers, not board geometry).
On play, places 1 food on each remaining even-numbered round space.
On play, places 1 food on each of the next 2 round spaces; prereq (exactly 2 occupations) is just a play-check.
Fires each time you obtain >=1 wood from an action space (obtain-keyed, source-restricted); schedules 1 food on the next round space.
Each harvest feeding phase, mandatory 1/2 food if living in a clay/stone house (plain state read); food arrives in time to feed, so before payment.
Fires immediately after ANY player (own or opponent) builds >=1 wood room (build-room sub-action); mandatory 1 food.
Fires on the sow sub-action; payout depends on which good types were sown (event payload), so needs the sow's provenance.
Two hooks: each use of Fishing (space, T-BEFORE default) adds 1 food to a pile on the card; before each play-occupation sub-action you get food equal to the pile.
Play cost forks (1 wood OR 1 clay). Hook 1: after each build-room, add 1 food to the card's pile; hook 2: at the start of each feeding phase, gain food equal to the pile.
Optional ('you can') conversion of wild boar to 4 food each, fired when you obtain boar outside the breeding phase (obtain-keyed with a phase exclusion).
Fires on playing/building an improvement; optional extra payment of 1 building resource of a type in the printed cost for 3 food — needs the built card's printed-cost payload (ST-PROV).
Each use of Fishing (T-BEFORE default) grants 4/3/2/1 bonus food keyed on the food count currently on the space — read before collection, so no snapshot needed.
On play seeds a 4-food pile on the card; at each work-phase start (round start), if you are last in turn order, move 1 food from the pile to your supply.
Fires each time you bake >=1 grain (bake sub-action); bonus food is conditional on the outcome and on plain state reads (own sheep/cattle), so after the bake resolves.
Optional choice at each harvest start; substitutes 1 food for your field+breeding phases — needs a per-harvest skip flag and overrides both field-yield and breeding for that harvest.
'Each time you use Day Laborer' -> before the space's action; food scales with a named space's good count (a state read, not board geometry), capped at 4.
On play +1 food; a standing payment rule letting 1 building resource substitute for up to 2 food of an occupation's play cost (currency substitution -> cost mod + legality extension).
Mandatory 4 food at the start of every harvest; the 2-grain-field prereq is only a play prerequisite.
Once per round, on your turn, replaces the person-placement with a granted Bake Bread (no worker placed); the all-players-have-a-person condition is a plain state check.
Adds a baking rate (exactly 1 grain -> 6 food, passive rule for any Bake Bread) plus an immediate optional Bake Bread sub-action on play.
Same shape as Iron Oven: passive bake rate (1 grain -> 3 food) plus an immediate optional Bake Bread on play.
One-time grain scaled by completed rounds — a plain state read at play time; no ongoing mechanic.
Fires on ANOTHER player's use of Forest (opponent-action hook), automatic 1 grain.
'Each time you use Grain Seeds' -> before; bonus grain scales with your baking-improvement count (plain state read).
The card is itself a field, but sown/harvested with WOOD (a building resource as a crop — exotic); harvesting the last wood off it auto-grants 1 vegetable in the field phase.
Card-field restricted to vegetables; harvesting its last vegetable grants an optional plow-1-field sub-action.
Card-field; removing its last grain/veg (normally the field-harvest) grants an optional immediate sow of the OTHER crop onto the card — needs which crop was removed (event provenance).

Fires on your sow sub-action (optional 'you can'); places an extra planted good IN the field (crop manipulation, not loose goods); requires field-pasture orthogonal adjacency. Effect resolves with/after the sow into that field.
The card itself is a field (L-CARDFIELD, an always-on rule); plus an automatic harvest-field-phase hook: after harvesting >=1 good from it, gain 1 food.
Optional hook during each harvest field phase; changes the field-harvest RULE for one chosen field (harvest ALL its crops instead of 1) — crop/field-yield manipulation.

On play, move up to 5 fence pieces from supply onto the card (a persistent on-card pool); thereafter fences taken from the card are free when building — a passive free-fence source consumed over time.
Each time you use a wood accumulation space (default T-BEFORE), optionally return 1 stone for +3 wood — a triggered stone->wood exchange.
One-shot on play: optionally return up to 3 built stables from the farmyard for 3 wood each.
Automatic hook on obtaining reed/stone, but SCOPED to action-space sources only (note the source restriction); grants +1 clay.
One-time on-play exchange of up to 4 building resources for an equal number of others (single exchange, no same-type trades per clarification).
Automatic: after each sow sub-action (into >=1 field), gain 1 stone.
The card is a field that plants STONE (a building resource as a crop, sown/harvested at vegetable rates) and sows as though 3 fields while counting as 1 — a one-off stone-as-crop mechanic (EXOTIC).
The card is a shared action space; any user gains 1 of each building-resource type they already hold; an opponent using it must first pay the owner 1 food (mandatory toll, before resolving).
On play +1 food; plus an optional hook after each of your Day Laborer uses: exchange 1 building resource for another.
Automatic at the start of each harvest breeding phase: if you have an empty unfenced stable, gain 1 sheep (must be accommodated).
Always-on rule change: breed sheep with only 1 sheep (relaxes the 2-of-a-type breeding requirement); the card itself holds 1 sheep (new animal slot).
